texto: There weren't any promises of a future life together, either, but he didn't need to live like a monk anymore. Two years was more than enough time for soul-searching and penance. As long as Laney was close enough to touch, to breath in, to taste like his goddamn last meal, he would take whatever she offered and not ask any questions about what it might cost him when she inevitably left.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Zoe York: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “There weren't any promises of a future life together, either, but he didn't need to live like a m…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, heartache, romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de romance e sexy.

texto: Forgiveness means letting go of the hope for a better past. You won't be able to forgive yourself for not doing things differently until you stop wishing things were different
explicacao: Citação de Zoe York sobre temas ligados a amor. “Forgiveness means letting go of the hope for a better past. You won't be able to forgive yourself…” articula amor, forgiveness, love em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como love.

texto: She'd just had the best sex of her life, with an ex-boyfriend she'd spent the last decade pretending didn't exist, in his adorable half-finished, renovating-by-himself one room schoolhouse. Unsettled wouldn't even begin to describe how Laney should be feeling, and it didn't matter, because how she actually felt was pretty damn good.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Zoe York sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “She'd just had the best sex of her life, with an ex-boyfriend she'd spent the last decade pretend…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, romance, sexy, útil em momentos de termino e sexy.
